entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: The iconic five-cylinder D5 defined XC90 diesel performance, delivering strong torque and proven durability across first-gen models.
compatibleVariants: Momentum, SE, Sport, Executive, R-Design, Inscription
commonFailures: Injector wear after 80k miles causing rough idle and misfires
EGR cooler clogging triggering limp mode on short-trip driving
Swirl flap actuator failure on pre-2008 units

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Updated D5 variant with refined injection mapping and improved emissions compliance for mid-generation XC90s.
commonFailures: DPF clogging on urban-driven examples requiring regeneration or replacement
Turbo wastegate sticking causing boost pressure faults

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Final iteration of the five-cylinder D5 with Euro 5 compliance and enhanced fuel economy for late first-gen XC90s.
commonFailures: AdBlue system faults on late models requiring injector or pump replacement
High-pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starting when hot

commonFailures: Turbo wastegate sticking on high-mileage units causing loss of boost
EGR cooler internal leak triggering coolant loss and warning lights

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Lower-output Drive-E diesel option for entry-level XC90 variants with focus on fuel economy and urban usability.
commonFailures: EGR valve carbon buildup causing rough idle and reduced power
Intake manifold swirl flap mechanism wear on higher-mileage examples

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Twin-turbocharged inline-six delivering flagship performance for early XC90s, later replaced by more efficient Drive-E units.
compatibleVariants: T6 SE, T6 Sport, T6 Executive
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattling noise before catastrophic failure at 90k-120k miles
Turbo oil feed coking leading to bearing failure on neglected units

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated inline-six offering smooth power delivery and improved reliability over the twin-turbo T6 for mid-generation XC90s.

Oil consumption increase on high-mileage units from valve stem seal wear

commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing catastrophic engine damage if ignored
Oil pump drive gear wear leading to low oil pressure warnings

entries.history: Turbocharged four-cylinder Drive-E petrol bringing efficient performance to second-gen XC90s with broad torque delivery.
compatibleVariants: T5 Momentum, T5 R-Design, T5 Inscription, T5 Core, T5 Plus, T5 Ultra

commonProblems.h2: Volvo XC90 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volvo XC90's D5 diesel and T6 petrol engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with common issues including injector wear, timing chain tensioner failure, and turbocharger problems. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your XC90.

problems.h4: D5 Injector & EGR Failure - Why First-Gen XC90 Diesels Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC90 D5 Momentum, SE, Sport, Executive (2002-2011, D5244T & D5244T4)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure common-rail injectors wear internally causing poor atomisation; EGR cooler clogs with carbon restricting exhaust flow and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and clean EGR valve/cooler or replace with refurbished unit
repairOptions.longevity: Restores power if caught early; does not address injector wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 5 injectors with calibrated units, code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves misfires and rough idle; engine must be otherwise healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors, EGR delete option, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when repair costs exceed £2,500 or engine has other wear issues

problems.recommendation: On a first-gen XC90 worth £3,000-£8,000, injector replacement often makes sense if the engine is otherwise healthy and has under 150k miles. However, if the gearbox or angle gear also needs attention, a rebuilt engine with warranty typically offers better long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt D5244T engine prices for your Volvo XC90

problems.h4: T6 Timing Chain Failure - Catastrophic Risk on Early Petrol XC90s
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC90 T6 SE, Sport, Executive (2003-2008, B6304T)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner hydraulic pressure drops allowing chain to stretch; once slack exceeds tolerance, chain skips teeth causing valve-to-piston contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides, and chain before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught early via rattling noise; requires engine removal

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner design, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only realistic option once chain has skipped; prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise on cold start from your T6 XC90, act immediately - preventative tensioner replacement is far cheaper than engine replacement. Once the chain has skipped, replacement is the only viable option; a rebuilt unit with upgraded components is more cost-effective than dealer repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for B6304T Volvo XC90 engine replacement now

problems.h4: Drive-E Turbo Wastegate Failure - Boost Loss on Modern XC90s
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup causes turbo wastegate actuator to stick open or closed; results in loss of boost, fault codes, and limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace electronic wastegate actuator, recalibrate via VIDA diagnostics
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost if turbo internals are healthy; common first-step repair

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned turbo with new actuator, oil lines, and gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses internal bearing wear; suitable if engine compression is good

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when turbo failure caused secondary damage or engine has high mileage

problems.recommendation: For Drive-E XC90s under 100k miles with no other issues, actuator or turbo replacement is usually cost-effective. However, if the engine has suffered oil starvation or has compression loss, a rebuilt unit with warranty offers better protection against repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare Drive-E engine prices for your Volvo XC90

problems.group: V8 Timing Chain & Oil Pump Issues
problems.h4: V8 Timing Chain & Oil Pump Issues - Flagship XC90 Reliability Concerns
problems.affectedModels: Volvo XC90 V8 SE, Sport, Executive, R-Design (2005-2011, B8444S)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner hydraulic pressure loss combined with oil pump drive gear wear; leads to chain skip and catastrophic valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chains, tensioners, guides, and oil pump drive gear
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught via rattling noise before damage occurs; complex job

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt V8 with upgraded tensioners and oil pump, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Only realistic option once damage has occurred; rebuilt units include all critical upgrades

problems.recommendation: Given the complexity and cost of V8 timing chain work, preventative replacement is rarely cost-effective on an XC90 worth £5,000-£12,000. If chain failure has occurred, a rebuilt engine with warranty is the only sensible path forward.
problems.cta: Get quotes for B8444S Volvo XC90 V8 engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volvo XC90
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with D5 injector wear, T6 timing chain failure, or Drive-E turbo issues, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volvo XC90 specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. With nationwide delivery and supply & fit options available, you can get back on the road without paying main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volvo XC90 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volvo XC90 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Volvo XC90 Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel dominates XC90 replacement enquiries, with the five-cylinder D5 (2002-2014) and four-cylinder Drive-E D4/D5 (2015-present) representing the highest volume of UK searches.
items.descriptor: Diesel dominates XC90 replacement enquiries, with the five-cylinder D5 (2002-2014) and four-cylinder Drive-E D4/D5 (2015-present) representing the highest volume of UK searches.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volvo XC90 Diesel Engine Families
families: D5244T - most common replacement engine (Momentum/SE), injector and EGR failure risk
D5244T4 - updated D5 variant (2006-2011), DPF clogging on urban-driven examples
D4204T14 - Drive-E twin-turbo diesel (2015-2020), turbo wastegate sticking common
foundIn: First generation PW chassis (2002-2014) - D5244T family
Second generation YV5 chassis (2015-present) - D4204T family
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and towing capability
D5 injector wear after 80k miles requiring replacement
EGR cooler clogging triggering limp mode on short-trip cars
High UK replacement demand on D5 Momentum and R-Design variants

items.title: Volvo XC90 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol XC90s span turbo five-cylinder, inline-six and Drive-E four-cylinder units, with T6 timing chain issues and V8 complexity driving replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: Petrol XC90s span turbo five-cylinder, inline-six and Drive-E four-cylinder units, with T6 timing chain issues and V8 complexity driving replacement enquiries.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volvo XC90 Petrol Engine Families
families: B6304T - twin-turbo T6 (2003-2008), timing chain tensioner failure risk
B6324S - naturally aspirated 3.2L (2007-2014), more reliable but still ages
B4204T11 - Drive-E turbo petrol (2015-2020), turbo wastegate issues common
foundIn: First generation PW chassis (2003-2014) - 2.5T, 3.2, T6, V8
Second generation YV5 chassis (2015-present) - T5, T6 Drive-E
knownFor: T6 twin-turbo performance but timing chain vulnerability
V8 flagship power but complex and expensive to repair
Drive-E efficiency with occasional turbo actuator faults
Higher replacement costs than diesel equivalents

items.title: Volvo XC90 Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: T8 Twin Engine plug-in hybrid combines Drive-E petrol with electric motor for flagship performance, while B5/B6 mild hybrids add 48V assistance to petrol units.
items.descriptor: T8 Twin Engine plug-in hybrid combines Drive-E petrol with electric motor for flagship performance, while B5/B6 mild hybrids add 48V assistance to petrol units.
families: B4204T27 - T8 Twin Engine PHEV (2016-2024), petrol + electric rear axle
B420T27 - B5 Mild Hybrid (2020-2024), 48V belt-driven starter-generator
B420T35 - B6 Mild Hybrid (2020-2024), higher-output 48V system
T8: Petrol engine drives front wheels, electric motor powers rear axle for AWD and electric-only range
B5/B6: 48V system provides torque assist, enables start-stop and energy recovery
Battery health inspection recommended during any engine replacement procedure
importantNotes: T8 replacement requires hybrid system specialist diagnosis and high-voltage safety protocols
Mild hybrid units need 48V battery and DC-DC converter inspection during engine swap
Availability more limited than standard engines; allow extra lead time for sourcing

engineYears.h2: Volvo XC90 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Volvo XC90 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original XC90 launched with five-cylinder diesel and petrol options, establishing the D5 as the volume seller and T6 as the performance flagship.
keyChanges: XC90 debut with PW chassis, seven-seat luxury SUV positioning
D5244T five-cylinder diesel introduced as primary powertrain
B5254T2 turbo petrol and B6294T twin-turbo petrol options available
Aisin automatic gearbox with AWD as standard on most variants
knownFor: D5 injector reliability concerns on earliest 2002-03 units
T6 timing chain tensioner wear emerging as key failure pattern
Robust chassis and safety reputation driving strong residual values
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2006 Volvo XC90 replacement engines
years.ticker: XC90 D5 injector replacement • XC90 T6 timing chain failure • XC90 2.5T turbo problems

years.description: Mid-cycle refresh brought updated D5 variants and the introduction of the 3.2L inline-six and V8 petrol options alongside revised automatic transmissions.
keyChanges: D5244T4 and D5244T5 updated diesel codes with improved emissions
B6324S 3.2L naturally aspirated petrol replaces twin-turbo T6
B8444S 4.4L V8 introduced for flagship variants
TF-80SC automatic gearbox replaces earlier AW55-50SN unit
knownFor: D5 EGR cooler clogging becoming common on urban-driven examples
V8 timing chain tensioner issues emerging as costly failure pattern
Improved refinement and emissions compliance across updated engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2011 Volvo XC90 replacement engines
years.ticker: XC90 D5 EGR failure • XC90 V8 timing chain • XC90 3.2 oil consumption

years.description: Last production years of the original XC90 saw D5244T18 diesel with Euro 5 compliance and refined trim levels before second-generation launch.
keyChanges: D5244T18 final D5 variant with AdBlue and Euro 5 emissions
Inscription trim introduced as top-spec luxury variant
Minor interior and technology updates ahead of replacement model
knownFor: Late-model D5 units generally more reliable with updated injection mapping
V8 and 3.2 petrol engines becoming rare on UK roads as values depreciate
Strong demand for replacement D5 engines from high-mileage fleet examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2014 Volvo XC90 replacement engines

years.description: All-new YV5 chassis brought modular Drive-E four-cylinder engines, electrification options and significant technology upgrades across the XC90 range.
keyChanges: Complete platform change to Scalable Product Architecture (SPA)
Drive-E four-cylinder engines replace five- and six-cylinder units
D4204T14 diesel and B4204T11 petrol introduced with twin-turbo technology
T8 Twin Engine plug-in hybrid variant launched with electric rear axle
popularModels: Volvo XC90 T8 Inscription Twin Engine
knownFor: Drive-E turbo wastegate issues emerging as common failure pattern
T8 hybrid complexity requiring specialist knowledge for replacement
Improved fuel economy but higher replacement costs for complex units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2019 Volvo XC90 replacement engines
years.ticker: XC90 D4 turbo failure • XC90 T8 hybrid engine • XC90 Drive-E wastegate

years.description: Latest XC90 updates integrate 48V mild hybrid technology across petrol variants while refining Drive-E diesel efficiency and emissions compliance.
keyChanges: B420T27 and B420T35 mild hybrid codes introduced with 48V belt-driven starter-generator
Updated infotainment and driver assistance systems across Core/Plus/Ultra trims
Continued production of T8 Twin Engine PHEV alongside new mild hybrid options
popularModels: Volvo XC90 B5 Plus Mild Hybrid AWD
Volvo XC90 B6 Inscription Mild Hybrid AWD
Volvo XC90 T8 Ultimate Twin Engine
knownFor: Mild hybrid system adds complexity but improves fuel economy and refinement
Replacement demand still dominated by first-gen D5 and second-gen D4 units
T8 PHEV replacement requires high-voltage specialist handling and diagnostics
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2024 Volvo XC90 replacement engines

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Volvo XC90 was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Volvo XC90 specialists.

faq.h2: Volvo XC90 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Volvo XC90 engine in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Volvo XC90 engine prices range from £1,800 for a D5 diesel to £4,500+ for T6 or V8 units, with supply & fit adding £800-£1,800 depending on workshop location and engine complexity.
keyPoints: D5244T rebuilt engines: £1,800-£3,500 supply only
Drive-E D4/T5 rebuilt: £2,200-£4,000 supply only
T6/V8 specialist rebuilds: £2,500-£7,000 supply only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £800-£1,800
All marketplace units include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Volvo XC90 engine prices now

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Volvo XC90 engine?
items.answer: Replace if repair costs exceed £2,500 or the engine has multiple issues; repair may make sense for isolated failures like a single injector on an otherwise healthy D5 under 120k miles.
keyPoints: Injector replacement viable if compression good and under 120k miles
Timing chain failure on T6/V8 always requires full replacement
Turbo failure on Drive-E often warrants rebuild if oil starvation occurred
Consider vehicle value: replacement rarely sensible on XC90 worth under £3k
Rebuilt engine with warranty offers peace of mind vs partial repair
items.cta: Get a Volvo XC90 engine replacement quote today

items.question: What causes Volvo XC90 D5 engine failure?
items.answer: D5 failures typically stem from injector wear after 80k miles, EGR cooler clogging on short-trip driving, or swirl flap mechanism failure on pre-2008 units.
keyPoints: Injector wear causes rough idle, misfires and increased fuel consumption
EGR cooler clogging triggers limp mode and power loss
Swirl flap actuator failure can damage intake manifold on early models
Regular motorway driving reduces DPF and EGR-related issues
Upgraded injectors and EGR delete options available on rebuilt units
items.cta: Compare D5244T engine prices for your XC90

items.question: Is the Volvo XC90 T6 timing chain failure common?
items.answer: Yes, B6304T T6 engines commonly suffer timing chain tensioner failure between 90k-120k miles, often causing catastrophic valve damage if the rattling noise is ignored.
keyPoints: Listen for cold-start rattling - early warning sign of tensioner wear
Preventative replacement costs £700-£1,300 at specialists
Once chain skips, only full engine replacement is viable
Rebuilt T6 engines include upgraded tensioner design to prevent repeat failure
Budget £2,500-£4,500 for rebuilt B6304T supply only
items.cta: Get quotes for B6304T Volvo XC90 engine replacement

items.question: Are used Volvo XC90 engines reliable?
items.answer: Used engines can be reliable if sourced from low-mileage accident-damaged donors with verified compression reports, but carry higher risk than rebuilt units with warranty.
keyPoints: Always request compression test report before purchasing used unit
Verify service history and mileage documentation from donor vehicle
Used D5 engines typically £1,200-£2,800 vs £1,800-£3,500 rebuilt
Rebuilt units include new wear items and 12-month warranty protection
Core charge may apply for exchange-basis used engine supply
items.cta: Check used Volvo XC90 engine availability now

items.question: How long does a Volvo XC90 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines can be delivered in 1-3 working days; full supply & fit typically requires 2-4 days including removal, installation, coding and road test at a specialist workshop.
keyPoints: Next-day delivery available on common D5244T and Drive-E codes
Complex units like T8 Twin Engine may require 3-5 days sourcing
Supply & fit labour typically 8-12 hours depending on variant
Post-installation ECU coding and adaptation required on Drive-E units
Road test and diagnostics included in specialist fitting service
items.cta: Find Volvo XC90 engine supply & fit deals

items.question: What warranty comes with Volvo XC90 replacement engines?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at any UK workshop, with some specialists offering extended terms.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across marketplace
Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK garage, not just fitting specialist
Rebuilt units include upgraded components addressing known failure points
Keep service records and use correct oil specs to maintain warranty validity
Confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before order placement
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC90 engine warranty options

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Volvo XC90?
items.answer: Engine swaps are technically possible but rarely cost-effective due to ECU coding, emissions compliance and insurance implications; sticking with the original engine code is strongly recommended.
keyPoints: ECU and immobiliser coding tied to original engine code and VIN
Emissions testing may fail if engine type doesn't match V5C registration
Insurance could be voided if non-standard engine not declared and approved
Rebuilt original-code engines available at competitive prices
Specialist advice essential before considering any engine conversion
items.cta: Get quotes for your exact Volvo XC90 engine code

items.question: Where can I get a Volvo XC90 engine fitted in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket's network includes over 100 vetted UK Volvo specialists offering supply & fit services nationwide, with mobile fitting available in some regions for D5 and Drive-E units.
keyPoints: Search by postcode to find nearest vetted Volvo XC90 specialist
Supply & fit quotes include removal, installation, fluids and diagnostics
Mobile fitting available for common D5244T and Drive-E codes in select areas
All fitting specialists carry public liability and guarantee workmanship
Book fitting after engine supply confirmed to coordinate delivery and installation
items.cta: Find Volvo XC90 engine fitting specialists near you

items.question: Do Volvo XC90 engines last?
items.answer: Well-maintained D5 diesel engines regularly exceed 200k miles; T6 petrol and V8 units require stricter servicing but can also reach high mileage with proactive timing chain and turbo maintenance.
keyPoints: D5 engines with full service history often reach 200k+ miles reliably
T6/V8 require timing chain inspection at 80k miles to prevent catastrophic failure
Drive-E units benefit from updated components but need quality oil and regular changes
Short-trip urban driving accelerates DPF/EGR issues on diesel variants
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components can extend vehicle life significantly
items.cta: Compare long-life rebuilt Volvo XC90 engine options
